relations: Sharks use their olfactory system to detect traces of blood and chemicals in the water, aiding in their hunting
Sharks rely on their lateral line system to detect vibrations and movements in the water
Sharks have ampullae of Lorenzini to detect electrical signals from other organisms
Great White Shark is a specific example of sharks, sharing the common sensory adaptations that make sharks successful predators
The tapetum lucidum is a structure found in sharks like the great white shark, improving their night vision
Sharks use the lateral line system to detect prey through changes in water pressure and movement
